# yahoo-finance-ai-tool

> Async Yahoo Finance client, CLI (`yfin`), and MCP server for AI coding
> agents. Fetches quotes, OHLCV history, fundamentals, key stats, technical
> indicators, dividends/splits/earnings, news, option chains, holders, and
> analyst data for any ticker on any world exchange (`AAPL`, `RELIANCE.NS`,
> `7203.T`, `BMW.DE`, etc.). No credentials required — cookie+crumb auth is
> bootstrapped automatically.

This file gives an AI coding agent everything needed to install this tool and
start using it autonomously, with no further human input.

## Install

```bash
pip install yahoo-finance-ai-tool
# or
uv pip install yahoo-finance-ai-tool
```

Verify:

```bash
yfin auth-status
```

## Register as an MCP server (recommended for agents)

After install, register the `yfinance` MCP server (stdio transport) with
your agent host. For Claude Code:

```bash
claude mcp add yfinance -- yfin serve
```

For other MCP-compatible hosts, point the client at the command `yfin serve`
(stdio) or run `yfin serve --transport http --port 8632` and connect to
`http://127.0.0.1:8632/mcp`.

Once registered, the following tools are available — call them directly,
no further setup:

`yfin_login`, `yfin_auth_status`, `yfin_search`, `yfin_quote`, `yfin_price`,
`yfin_fundamentals`, `yfin_key_stats`, `yfin_profile`, `yfin_technical`,
`yfin_events`, `yfin_earnings`, `yfin_news`, `yfin_options`, `yfin_holders`,
`yfin_analysts`, `yfin_snapshot`, `yfin_bulk`, `yfin_export`.

## Or use the CLI directly (no MCP host)

Every command mirrors an MCP tool 1:1. Add `--json` for machine-readable
output.

```bash
yfin search "reliance industries"            # resolve name -> symbol, any exchange
yfin quote AAPL MSFT RELIANCE.NS              # realtime quotes
yfin price AAPL --range 1y --interval 1d      # OHLCV history (intraday: 1m..1h)
yfin fundamentals AAPL --statement income --frequency annual
yfin stats AAPL                               # valuation ratios, margins, growth
yfin profile AAPL                             # sector/industry/summary
yfin technical AAPL --range 1y                # SMA/EMA/RSI/MACD/BB/ATR/Stoch + signals
yfin events AAPL --range 5y                   # dividends, splits, earnings calendar
yfin earnings AAPL                            # EPS actual vs estimate history
yfin news AAPL --limit 10
yfin options AAPL --expiry 2026-12-18         # option chain
yfin holders AAPL                             # institutional/fund holders
yfin analysts AAPL                            # price targets + recommendation trend
yfin snapshot AAPL                            # everything in one JSON
yfin bulk AAPL MSFT RELIANCE.NS TCS.NS --dataset price --range 5y --out prices.parquet
yfin export AAPL --what snapshot --fmt json
```

## Symbol conventions

Use Yahoo Finance ticker suffixes: `AAPL`/`MSFT` (US, no suffix),
`RELIANCE.NS` (NSE India), `RELIANCE.BO` (BSE), `7203.T` (Tokyo Stock
Exchange), `BMW.DE` (Xetra). If unsure of a symbol, call `yfin_search` /
`yfin search "<company name>"` first to resolve it.

## Auth / self-healing

Cookie+crumb session is bootstrapped automatically on first use and persists
at `~/.yfinance-ai/session.json` — there is nothing to configure. If a tool
returns an `AuthenticationError` or `RateLimitError`-shaped error:

1. Call `yfin_login` (MCP) or run `yfin login` (CLI) to refresh the session.
2. Retry the original call.
3. For `yfin_bulk`/`yfin bulk` on large symbol lists, lower `concurrency`
   (e.g. `4`) if throttled.

## Conventions agents should follow

- Missing numeric fields are `None`, never `0` — treat as "not available",
  do not treat as zero in calculations.
- `yfin_bulk` isolates per-symbol failures as `{"error": ...}` entries; the
  batch never aborts — check each entry individually.
- Quarterly statement line items are deprecated by Yahoo (only `endDate`
  remains per period) — use annual statements or `yfin_key_stats` for
  current ratios.
- Options data is mostly US-only. Intraday intervals (`1m`..`1h`) only work
  for recent date ranges (Yahoo limitation).
- This is an **unofficial API** wrapping Yahoo's public endpoints — be polite
  (default rate limit 2 req/s) and handle occasional 429s via retry.

## Worked example: full equity analysis

```
yfin_quote(symbols=["AAPL"])
yfin_key_stats(symbol="AAPL")
yfin_fundamentals(symbol="AAPL", statement="income", frequency="annual")
yfin_technical(symbol="AAPL", range="1y")
yfin_analysts(symbol="AAPL")
yfin_news(symbol="AAPL", limit=5)
```

Synthesize: valuation (P/E vs growth), revenue/margin trend, technical
posture (RSI, MACD signal, price vs SMA200, ATR for volatility), analyst
consensus vs current price, and recent news catalysts.

## More

- Full README (architecture, endpoints, testing): https://github.com/singhvedant/yahoo-finance-ai-tool
- Source: https://github.com/singhvedant/yahoo-finance-ai-tool
